metadata.name: Manage the Boot Loader and Kernel Command Line
metadata.description: Reconfigure the boot loader to select different kernels and modify kernel command-line arguments using grubby
objectives: Reboot the system and examine the GRUB2 menu
View the current boot-loader configuration from the command line
Reconfigure the boot loader to select a different kernel as the default
prerequisites: Basic understanding of the Linux boot process
Familiarity with systemd and systemctl commands

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: Target server for boot loader configuration

tasks.name: Verify grubby command is available
tasks.description: The grubby utility should be installed and accessible
hints: The grubby package should be installed by default on RHEL systems

tasks.name: Verify default kernel is at index 1
tasks.description: The default kernel should be reverted back to index 1 (the most recent kernel)

checks.description: Check that default kernel index is 1

tasks.success_message: Default kernel is correctly set to index 1
tasks.failure_message: Default kernel is not at index 1
hints: Use 'grubby --set-default-index 1' to set the default kernel to index 1
Verify with 'grubby --default-index' to check the current default

tasks.name: Verify rhgb quiet arguments removed from older kernel
tasks.description: The rhgb quiet arguments should be removed from the kernel at index 0

checks.description: Check that rhgb quiet is not in kernel 0 args

tasks.success_message: Kernel arguments correctly reverted (rhgb quiet removed)
tasks.failure_message: The rhgb quiet arguments are still present on kernel at index 0
hints: Use 'grubby --update-kernel /boot/vmlinuz-<version> --remove-args="rhgb quiet"' to remove the arguments

tasks.name: Verify boot loader entries exist
tasks.description: Boot loader entry files should exist in /boot/loader/entries/

checks.description: Check that at least one boot loader entry exists

tasks.failure_message: No boot loader entries found in /boot/loader/entries/
hints: Boot loader entries should be in /boot/loader/entries/ directory
Each kernel should have a corresponding .conf file
